test_cases.prompt: Create a main window for a file manager application with a sidebar, toolbar, and file list view. It should support dark and light themes.
test_cases.expected_output: PyQt6 main window implementation following MVC pattern with signals/slots

assertions.check: Output uses pyqtSignal declarations and .connect() for inter-component communication instead of polling or QTimer-based checks
Output uses @property decorators for read-only state access rather than directly exposing public mutable attributes
Output uses QVBoxLayout, QHBoxLayout, or QGridLayout for widget positioning instead of absolute positioning (setGeometry)
Output calls super().__init__() in custom widget or window constructors
Output includes return type annotations (-> None, -> str, -> int, etc.) on public methods

test_cases.prompt: Create a settings dialog with fields for username, password, server URL, and connection timeout. Include validation and an apply button.
test_cases.expected_output: Modal dialog implementation with validation and signal-based result communication

assertions.check: Output creates a QDialog subclass with setModal(True) or uses exec() for modal behavior
Output includes input validation logic (checking empty fields, URL format, or timeout range) before accepting settings
Output uses a pyqtSignal to communicate changed settings to the parent rather than direct parent method calls
Output defines named constants (class-level or module-level) for default values like timeout ranges, minimum lengths, or port numbers instead of inline magic numbers
Output uses underscore-prefixed private attributes (self._username, self._timeout) rather than bare public attributes (self.username, self.timeout)

test_cases.prompt: Create a custom circular progress widget that shows download progress with smooth animation and color transitions.
test_cases.expected_output: Custom QWidget with QPropertyAnimation and paintEvent override

assertions.check: Output overrides paintEvent() to perform custom rendering with QPainter
Output uses QPropertyAnimation or QEasingCurve for smooth value transitions rather than instant value changes
Output declares a pyqtSignal (e.g., value_changed or progress_changed) that emits when the progress value changes
Output provides a public method (e.g., set_value, set_progress) for changing the value rather than allowing direct attribute assignment
Output sets a sizeHint(), minimumSize(), or setMinimumSize() to ensure the widget renders at a reasonable default size

test_cases.prompt: Create a system tray application that monitors clipboard changes and shows notifications when new URLs are copied.
test_cases.expected_output: QSystemTrayIcon-based application with clipboard monitoring and notifications

assertions.check: Output creates or uses QSystemTrayIcon for the tray icon
Output creates a QMenu with context menu actions for the tray icon (at minimum a quit/exit action)
Output connects to QApplication.clipboard().dataChanged signal rather than polling the clipboard with a timer
Output uses showMessage() on the QSystemTrayIcon for displaying notifications to the user
Output checks QSystemTrayIcon.isSystemTrayAvailable() before attempting to create the tray icon

test_cases.prompt: Create a themed button component with hover animation, configurable colors, and click ripple effect for a design tool application.
test_cases.expected_output: Custom QPushButton subclass with animation and theming support

assertions.check: Output overrides enterEvent and leaveEvent (or installEventFilter) to trigger hover animations
Output defines color values as class-level constants or configurable constructor parameters rather than inline hex strings in methods
Output uses QPropertyAnimation for color or size transitions rather than instant style changes
Output uses either setStyleSheet() with dynamic values or overrides paintEvent() for custom rendering
Output includes a file header comment with developer name, date, and purpose
